title: Migration, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, Operations, and Defense · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: A joint reader, editor, accessibility, security, platform, and operations review board. The board needs a reproducible immutable artifact, least-authority GitHub Pages pipeline, route and content migration diff, post-deploy smoke evidence, cache-aware rollback rehearsal, incident ownership, and a defensible record of residual risks and parser limitations. Planned artifact: Deliver Production Publication, Migration, and Recovery Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a…
objectives: Eliminate unstable order, paths, locale, time, random values, permissions, and environment leakage; use recorded source and tool identity and compare clean digests.
Pin workflow dependencies, minimize permissions, isolate untrusted changes, protect environments, retain logs safely, and separate build from deployment authority.
Verify representative URLs, headers, assets, fragments, accessibility tasks, canonical origin, cache behavior, artifact identity, and rollback trigger after publication.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Reproducible Build
steps.instruction:
project case — A project publishing team handles case 4779 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Eliminate unstable order, paths, locale, time, random values, permissions, and environment leakage; use recorded source and tool identity and compare clean digests.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-production-defense-1uzkfh" that uses case 3478 to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Production Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Production Defense into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-production-defense-1uzkfh
# Competency: ssg-production-defense.
# Case 3478: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_production_defense_1uzkfh(routes_before: int = 40, routes_after: int = 42, accounted_routes: int = 42, rollback_seconds: int = 90, recovery_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1uzkfh = "1uzkfh-hfkzu1-3478"
    assert evidence_variant_1uzkfh
    evidence_axes_1uzkfh = (
        "reader1uzkfh",
        "source3478",
        "parsehfkzu1",
        "route1uzkfh3478",
        "artifact34781uzkfh",
        "recoveryhfkzu13478",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1uzkfh)) == 6
    if routes_before < 1 or routes_after < 1:
        return False, routes_after, "migration-shape"
    if accounted_routes != routes_after:
        return False, accounted_routes, "route-diff-gap"
    if rollback_seconds < 0 or rollback_seconds > 300:
        return False, rollback_seconds, "rollback-budget"
    if not recovery_owner:
        return False, routes_after, "unowned-recovery"
    assert accounted_routes == routes_after
    return True, routes_after, "production-publication-defense"
